Title: Broad-based SME stimulus not needed for now, says Bank Negara
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has indicated that broad-based stimulus measures are not immediately necessary to support small and medium enterprises (SMEs), favouring targeted assistance instead.
Responding to calls from industry groups for government intervention amid geopolitical uncertainties, the central bank said Malaysia is entering the current environment from a "position of strength".
Speaking at Bank Negara's Annual Report 2025 press conference, governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said businesses have already undergone significant support measures following the pandemic.
These include fiscal assistance and policy interventions over the past five years that have strengthened resilience.
"Banks are ready to help borrowers, corporates and SMEs. If they face any financial issues, they can approach the banks. They will be ready to help," he said.
He added that many businesses, including SMEs, are now better positioned to navigate current challenges.
Any additional support will be assessed based on evolving conditions, with Bank Negara taking a holistic view of the economy to identify sectors that may require intervention.
"We cannot approach this matter on a silo and sector basis. It has to be holistic by looking at the overall state of the economy, then zooming in to see where we would need to place our resources," he said.
Abdul Rasheed said Bank Negara continues to engage industry players through regular meetings and platforms such as the National Economic Action Council, enabling authorities to better assess business conditions and tailor responses.
The central bank will also continue to monitor global developments closely and respond with appropriate measures to ensure businesses receive the necessary support without resorting to large-scale interventions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DoE raids illegal plastic recycling premises in Sungai Petani
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: The Kedah Department of Environment (DoE) raided a premises suspected of carrying out illegal plastic waste processing activities in the Taman Ria Jaya Industrial Area here yesterday.
Its director Sharifah Zakiah Syed Sahab said the operation, which began at 8.30am and concluded at 1pm, was conducted following continuous monitoring of industrial activities with the potential to pollute the environment.
She said inspections found the premises engaged in cleaning and extrusion of plastic waste to produce plastic pellets, and operating without the required Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) approval under the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
"The premises are believed to have been operating covertly to avoid detection by the public," she said in a statement today.
Sharifah Zakiah said the DoE issued an Equipment Operation Detention order under Section 38(1)(a) of the Act on a plastic extrusion machine, as well as an air pollution control system and an industrial effluent treatment system believed to have been used without DoE approval.
Further investigations revealed a large quantity of raw materials and finished products at the premises, including 700 jumbo bags, or about 350 metric tonnes, of plastic waste believed to be used as raw material.
Enforcement officers also discovered some 258 metric tonnes of finished products in the form of plastic pellets, packed in 10,320 sacks weighing about 25kg each.
"DoE also collected samples of plastic waste and pellets from the premises for laboratory analysis by the Chemistry department," she said.
The case is being investigated under Section 34A(6) of the Environmental Quality Act 1974 for carrying out solid waste recycling activities without EIA approval.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia seeks deeper Japan ties, boosts semiconductor push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is seeking deeper industrial cooperation with Japan, particularly in the semiconductor sector, Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Sim Tze Tzin said.
He said Malaysia is positioning itself as a stable and neutral base in Asean for Japanese companies looking to diversify operations, supported by political stability, strong rule of law and a wide network of trade agreements.
"Malaysia has signed 17 free trade agreements, making Malaysia a very neutral ground to access a very wide market in the world for Japanese companies," he said at the New Japan-Malaysia Industrial Cooperation Seminar today.
Sim noted that as of December 2025, a total of 2,872 manufacturing projects had been implemented in Malaysia with investments amounting to RM107.9 billion.
"Bilateral trade between Malaysia and Japan reached RM142.9 billion in 2025, with total trade continuing to expand by 2.3 per cent in January 2026 to RM12.8 billion.
"Against a backdrop of geopolitical tensions and shifting economic dynamics, Malaysia offers a strategically located and reliable base for Japanese investors seeking supply chain diversification," he said.
Sim added that Malaysia's industrial policy demonstrates strong alignment with Japan's strategic priorities, particularly across 17 strategic fields, including semiconductors, green transformation and economic security.
"With the launch of our National Semiconductor Strategy in May 2024, we are advancing into integrated circuit design, advanced packaging and semiconductor research and development. These opportunities also exist in lithography, a critical component of semiconductor manufacturing," he said.
He urged stronger collaboration in talent development through training programmes, cross-border internships and knowledge-sharing initiatives, while encouraging Japanese companies to deepen engagement with Malaysian SMEs.
"Develop local suppliers, and transfer not just technology but also the management practices that have made Japanese industry world-class," added Sim.
He added that Malaysia is opening up opportunities across high-growth sectors including speciality chemicals, advanced materials, medical devices, smart manufacturing, electric vehicles, carbon capture, aerospace and critical minerals.
Malaysia, he said, aims to establish 3,000 smart factories by 2030, driving demand for industrial IoT, AI-enabled systems and advanced automation technologies.
Meanwhile, Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz said Malaysia-Japan ties remain one of the region's most established economic partnerships.
He noted that Japanese firms helped build Malaysia's electrical and electronics industry in the 1970s, with investments expanding into chemicals, machinery, precision engineering, transport equipment and advanced semiconductor and digital sectors.
"As at the end of last year, Japan remains one of Malaysia's top investors, with RM142.9 billion in approved investments across more than 3,800 projects, creating around 500,000 jobs," he said.
Tengku Zafrul added that more than 2,800 projects have been implemented, reflecting Japanese companies' long-term commitment to Malaysia.
He said the partnership is now evolving towards higher-value collaboration, supported by Malaysia's industrial transformation agenda, including the New Industrial Master Plan, National Semiconductor Strategy and National Energy Transition Roadmap.
"We bring policy stability, institutional backing and hands-on facilitation. Mida stands as a full life-cycle partner for every Japanese investor," he said.
"First, consider going deeper on our shores. Second, Malaysia offers policy clarity and consistency.
"Third, the Mida-MUFG bank partnership and the Japanese Chamber of Trade & Industry, Malaysia network are built to open doors for Japanese SMEs in particular. So use these networks," he added.
Tengku Zafrul also highlighted conflict in West Asia has sent energy prices soaring, affecting chemicals and metals used in semiconductor production as well as regional supply chains.
"For Japanese companies, this is a moment that demands absolute clarity on where their regional supply chain anchors must be. Malaysia and Japan already have the links, the alignment and the history.
"Malaysia is not asking Japanese companies to gamble on potential but to bank on proof," he added, citing five decades of industrial cooperation between both countries.
That proof, he said, is built on a long-standing partnership that has stood the test of time, providing a strong foundation for Japanese firms to anchor their next growth phase in Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Miti clarifies BYD license terms, says rules apply to all investors
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (Miti) has clarified that conditions imposed on Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker BYD's proposed assembly operations in Malaysia are part of a broader, non-discriminatory policy framework.
The company's local unit, BYD Automotive Malaysia Sdn Bhd, was granted an interim manufacturing licence on Sept 29, 2025 to assemble energy efficient vehicles, including EVs and plug-in hybrid models, in Tanjung Malim, Perak.
Miti said the approval was based on an export-oriented production model aligned with national industrial policies.
"The licence is granted on the basis of an export-oriented production profile, where majority of output is destined for international markets to leverage Malaysia's free trade agreements and regional supply chains," it said in a statement.
Miti stressed that the conditions imposed, including export orientation, localisation requirements and pricing thresholds, are applied consistently to all new automotive investments and are not specific to BYD.
"These conditions are not unique to BYD but reflect a consistent approach applied to all new automotive investments in Malaysia," it said.
The clarification follows a news report that BYD may rethink its plans for a completely knocked down facility in Tanjung Malim due to conditions requiring up to 80 per cent of production to be exported and a minimum pricing threshold.
Miti said domestic sales are capped at 10,000 units annually, equivalent to 20 per cent of production capacity, describing it as "a pro-export condition aimed at ensuring that investment contributes to Malaysia's trade balance and global supply chain integration".
On pricing, the ministry said the minimum on-the-road (OTR) price for locally assembled BYD vehicles is set at RM100,000, not RM200,000 as reported.
"The condition imposed for the domestic market is a minimum OTR price of RM100,000. This ensures local assembly focuses on higher-value segments, preserving market space for national players while still offering affordable EV solutions," it said.
The ministry added that localisation requirements, including in-country body shop, paint and trim operations, are intended to promote high-value manufacturing activities and integration with the local supply chain.
It said the approach is "not protectionist but developmental", aimed at ensuring foreign investments contribute to technology transfer, local vendor development and long-term industrial growth.
"Investments without clear export commitments or localisation plans offer limited benefit to Malaysia's automotive ecosystem," it said.
Miti said Malaysia remains open to foreign automakers, including Chinese brands, noting that multiple global players have established or are expanding operations in the country under similar policy conditions.
"It is Malaysia's genuine desire to welcome world-class manufacturers including BYD, not only for vehicle assembly but to participate across the full automotive value chain," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia condemns attack on Unifil, extends condolences to Indonesia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has conveyed its condolences to the family and the Government of Indonesia following an incident that killed one peacekeeper and injured three others serving under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil).
In a statement, the Foreign Ministry said any attack on a Unifil peacekeeping mission constituted a serious violation of international humanitarian law and United Nations Security Council Resolution 1701 (2006).
"Malaysia calls on all parties to fully comply with their obligations under international law, including the implementation of United Nations Security Council Resolution 1701 (2006) and other relevant UN resolutions, to ensure the safety of UN peacekeepers and the protection of civilians and civilian infrastructure," it said.
The ministry also stressed that those responsible must be held accountable.
In a related development, the Foreign Ministry said personnel from the Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) currently serving under the Unifil mission were safe.
On Monday, Indonesia confirmed that one of its peacekeepers was killed while three others were injured after indirect artillery fire struck a Unifil post in southern Lebanon.
According to Indonesia's Foreign Ministry, the incident occurred near Adchit al-Qusayr on Sunday night (March 29), amid ongoing hostilities between the Israel Defence Forces and armed groups in the area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yeo's cuts Singapore jobs as production shifts to Malaysia on rising costs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: HOME-grown beverage giant Yeo Hiap Seng is laying off 25 employees at its Senoko facility - about nine per cent of its Singapore workforce - as it consolidates its canned food manufacturing operations in Malaysia.
"This consolidation enables the group's Johor and Selangor facilities to optimise capacity utilisation and strengthen overall manufacturing efficiency across its network," said the mainboard-listed company in a Singapore stock exchange filing on Tuesday,
The move comes as Singapore's food and beverage sector grapples with rising input costs, driven by higher energy, logistics and raw material prices, alongside geopolitical tensions linked to the Iran conflict.
At the same time, the company is facing softer consumer demand and intensifying competition. For the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025, Yeo's posted an 11 per cent decline in revenue to S$292.4 million, compared to S$328.6 million a year earlier.
Despite the weaker top line, net profit rose sharply to S$21.1 million from S$6.9 million previously, supported by tighter cost controls and improved operational efficiency.
Yeo's said the Senoko layoffs were communicated during a closed-door briefing at its 3 Senoko Way facility.
The company added that it "deeply regrets" the decision and is providing affected employees with job placement assistance, career guidance and counselling support in collaboration with the Food, Drinks and Allied Workers Union (FDAWU) under NTUC.
Despite the workforce reduction, the Senoko site will continue to function as Yeo's headquarters, cross-border logistics hub, and limited-scale production centre.
Founded in 1901 by Yeo Keng Lian, the company began as Hiap Seng Sauce Factory in Fujian, China, before relocating to Singapore in the 1930s and expanding into canned foods and beverages. It has since grown into a global brand with production facilities across Singapore, Malaysia and China, exporting to more than 55 countries worldwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kota Setar, Pendang schools to resume face-to-face classes tomorrow
Article:
ALOR STAR: School sessions in Kota Setar and Pendang will resume face-to-face tomorrow after both districts recorded Level 1 (Alert) temperature readings of between 35 degrees Celsius and 37 degrees Celsius since yesterday.
Kedah Education Department (JPN) director Abdul Rahim Mat said the department continuously monitored temperature readings issued by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) before deciding on the implementation of home-based teaching and learning (PdPR) in the state.
"In the past few days, rainfall has helped lower temperatures to Level 1. Previously, it was at Level 2, which was why PdPR sessions were implemented to safeguard students from the heat," he said at a press conference following the announcement of the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results for Kedah today.
He added that with temperatures now easing, schools would resume normal operations, although all activities would be conducted in line with guidelines issued to schools, parents and students.
"Students must be protected from the heat and encouraged to stay hydrated. Overall, the PdPR sessions ran smoothly, although some students had to share devices with their parents.
"Despite these challenges, the learning process continued effectively, with only the location and timing of lessons adjusted," he said.
A total of 86,030 students — comprising 65,634 from 154 schools in Kota Setar and 20,396 from 52 schools in Pendang — took part in PdPR over three days starting Sunday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Villagers build sandbags as Golok River dries up in drought
Article:
TANAH MERAH: Residents along the Malaysia–Thailand border are building sandbag barriers on the Golok River to retain water as drought leaves levels dangerously low.
With the river dropping to knee height, villagers must rely on it for bathing, washing, and collecting water for daily needs.
Tengku Abdul Rahman, 46, said his village has not received rain since early this year, and the situation "is the worst in three years."
Life revolves around the river after his home has been without a water supply for three months.
Normally, the Golok River is about five metres deep, but the prolonged drought has left it extremely shallow.
The father of seven goes to the river every morning, about 150m from his house, to wash clothes, bathe, and collect water for ablution.
"Everything we do is at the river — from bathing and washing clothes to collecting water for prayers," he said at the riverbank yesterday.
[[nid:1407237]]
He added that villagers had recently begun placing sandbags to retain water.
Another resident, Mohd Imran Mohd Nor, 36, said the river is now so shallow they can wade across to the other side.
"Everything is done at the river, including bathing. We hope the sandbag barriers will help us," he said.
Trader Mohd Fakrul Ridzwan, 24, said the drought, which began during the fasting month, had forced villagers to dig wells with little success.
"Even digging wells is difficult now. Water is getting scarcer. We hope for water supply assistance as soon as possible," he said.
The Golok River, which forms part of the Malaysia–Thailand border in Kelantan, is an important source of irrigation for farming and rural livelihoods.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SPM 2025 leavers urged to join PISMP to become teachers by 2030
Article:
KAJANG: Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 leavers who meet the entry requirements are encouraged to continue their studies through the Bachelor of Education (PISMP) programme at Teacher Education Institutes (IPG) nationwide.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said applications for the 2026 intake were still open and will close this Thursday.
He added that this year's intake quota for PISMP at IPGs had been increased to 9,800, compared with around 5,000 to 6,000 recruits over the past two years.
"We are accelerating this recruitment to meet demand in the teaching field. For Sekolah Menengah Jenis Kebangsaan (SMJK), we, together with Dong Zong (United Chinese School Committees' Association) and Jiao Zong (United Chinese School Teachers' Association), are actively promoting this opportunity.
"Through this programme, SPM graduates who enter IPG will undergo a five-year course of study and, by 2030 or 2031, will be able to graduate as teachers for national schools as well as Chinese and Tamil SMJK," he said.
Wong told reporters this after the announcement of the SPM 2025 results at SMJK Yu Hua here today.
According to the PISMP application guidelines issued by the Education Ministry, candidates must pass the SPM with excellent grades in any five subjects and obtain at least a credit in Bahasa Melayu, English and History.
Other requirements include being no older than 20 years of age on June 30, 2026, being physically fit, and actively participating in co-curricular activities.
At the same press conference, Wong also said that the SPM 2025 results for the Chinese Language showed a one per cent improvement from last year, with 40,888 of the 46,046 candidates achieving credits from A to C.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Korean Air to shift to emergency mode in April amid rising oil prices from Iran war
Article:
SEOUL: Korean Air will enter emergency management mode from April as rising oil prices driven by the war in the Middle East weigh on costs, a source with knowledge of the matter told Reuters on Tuesday.
The airline said in an internal memo reviewed by Reuters that if high oil prices persist, it expects significant disruption to its annual business targets, and will shift to an emergency operating system from April.
The memo added that the airline plans to implement phased response measures based on oil price levels, and step up company-wide cost efficiency to offset surging fuel costs.
T'way Air and Asiana Airlines have also entered emergency management mode this month amid rising fuel costs, according to local media reports, underscoring the broader strain on South Korea's aviation industry.
Korean Air said it expects fuel costs for April to rise to around 450 US cents per gallon, significantly higher than the 220 US cents per gallon cited in its business plan, a sharp increase in its monthly cost burden.
Fuel surcharges for flights departing from South Korea in April are also set to jump, with fares on Incheon to New York and Incheon to Chicago routes rising by more than 200 per cent, while surcharges on Incheon to London and Incheon to Paris routes are expected to increase by nearly 250 per cent, according to Korean Air's website.
Korean Air shares were trading down 1.9 per cent, compared with the benchmark KOSPI's 1.9 per cent fall at 0251 GMT.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara: Malaysian banks well-capitalised, can withstand crisis-level shocks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's financial system remains robust and well-positioned to support the economy despite global uncertainties, Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said.
He said banks and insurers in Malaysia continue to operate with strong capital positions and ample liquidity.
He noted that stress tests conducted by the central bank indicate that the banking system can withstand scenarios more severe than the global financial crisis and the 2020 pandemic.
"This means banks have the buffers to absorb losses, and, even in challenging conditions, they can continue lending and supporting financial intermediation.
"Insurers also demonstrate the ability to withstand shocks while providing protection to households and businesses," he said at a press conference following the release of Bank Negara's flagship publications.
Abdul Rasheed said households and businesses in Malaysia maintain sound repayment capacity, with low levels of risky or impaired loans.
While some micro, small and medium enterprises face localised challenges, overall debt-servicing ratios remain stable.
He said credit flows remain intact, supporting working capital and investment needs, reflecting the financial system's role in facilitating growth rather than amplifying economic stress.
Beyond its resilience, Abdul Rasheed said the financial sector is evolving to address current and future challenges.
He said the central bank has focused on reforms to enhance risk protection in insurance, advance value-based finance to promote sustainable and inclusive growth, modernise payment systems for safer and more convenient transactions and strengthen safeguards against fraud and illicit activity.
In 2025 alone, preventive measures helped avert RM1.2 billion in potential fraud losses.
"Malaysia has a financial system that does not just withstand shocks but also enables growth, protects trust and evolves with the needs of people and the economy," he said.
Abdul Rasheed said Bank Negara is working on its next Financial Sector Blueprint, which will aim to catalyse resilient growth, strengthen foundational structures and provide clear guidance on innovation and inclusion.
He said the nation's financial system does not merely withstand shocks but actively supports growth, safeguards trust and evolves to meet the needs of the people and the economy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hot weather: Mobile pumps deployed to Perak padi fields
Article:
IPOH: Mobile water pumps have been deployed as a temporary measure to ensure an adequate water supply in several critical padi cultivation areas in Perak amid the ongoing hot weather.
State Rural Development, Plantation, Agriculture and Food Industry Committee chairman Datuk Zolkafly Harun said the deployment covered padi fields within the Seberang Perak Irrigation Scheme, which spans 8,418ha.
"Mobile water pumps are currently maintaining water supply in the affected areas, but their long-term effectiveness will depend on existing water sources, particularly rivers," he said.
He added that a total of 5,561 farmers under the Seberang Perak Integrated Agricultural Development Area (IADA) had been affected by the current heatwave impacting several states.
Zolkafly said this involved two main schemes: the Seberang Perak Irrigation Scheme and the Sungai Manik/Labu Kubong Irrigation Scheme, covering 4,938.07ha.
He also said that the Seberang Perak IADA had submitted requests to borrow additional mobile water pumps from relevant agencies to ensure all affected areas receive sufficient water supply.
"Seberang Perak IADA is fully committed to ensuring adequate water supply so that agricultural activities in the affected areas can continue without disruption," he said.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has forecast that the hot weather is expected to persist until the early phase of the Southwest Monsoon in June, due to drier atmospheric conditions and lower rainfall.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai woman caught smuggling 51kg of subsidised cooking oil in car spare tyre
Article:
KOTA BARU: A Thai woman's attempt to smuggle 51kg of Malaysian subsidised cooking oil, concealed in a vehicle's spare tyre compartment, was foiled when she was arrested yesterday.
The woman, who was driving a car, was detained by enforcement officers at the Rantau Panjang Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complex.
Kelantan Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry deputy director Aswadi Jaafar said the woman was stopped at 2.15pm during Ops Tiris.
"The enforcement officers spotted a foreign-registered Mazda 2 driven by the woman and, upon inspecting the vehicle, discovered the cooking oil believed to be intended for smuggling into a neighbouring country.
"The 51kg of 1kg cooking oil packets in various brands, valued at RM227, were neatly concealed beneath the lining of the spare tyre compartment in the car boot, likely in an attempt to evade detection at the border," he said.
Aswadi said that the woman was detained for further investigation and the goods seized.
He said authorities would also determine the source of the subsidised cooking oil obtained by the suspect.
"The ministry will not tolerate any parties involved, including wholesalers or retailers found to be colluding in the misappropriation of subsidised goods. Action will be taken against those abusing government subsidies," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 21 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961, read together with Regulation 3(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974 and Regulation 3 of the Control of Supplies (Prohibition of Export) Regulations 2022.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrade, Responsible Business Alliance partner to advance ESG-driven trade
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: THE Malaysia External Trade Development Corp (Matrade) has partnered with the Responsible Business Alliance (RBA) to advance responsible and sustainable trade practices.
The collaboration was formalised through the signing of a memorandum of understanding (MoU), signalling a shared commitment to integrating environmental, social and governance (ESG) principles within international trade.
By leveraging their respective networks and expertise, Matrade and RBA will work together to enhance awareness, facilitate timely information sharing and strengthen the understanding of evolving global sustainability requirements among stakeholders.
According to Matrade, the partnership underscores Malaysia's proactive approach in aligning with international standards and reinforcing its position as a responsible trading nation, as global trade continues to be shaped by increasingly stringent sustainability expectations.
The collaboration will also support broader outreach efforts to industry players, encouraging greater adoption of responsible business practices across global supply chains.
The RBA, as one of the world's largest industry coalitions dedicated to responsible business conduct, brings together leading global companies across key sectors, including the electronics, retail, automotive and toy industries, all committed to improving sustainability outcomes across global supply chains.
Through this partnership, Matrade will further strengthen its engagement with international stakeholders and amplify Malaysia's sustainability narrative in key export markets.
The MoU also serves as a framework for collaboration between Matrade and RBA, signalling a mutual intention to support sustainable trade development and contribute to a more responsible global trading ecosystem.
Matrade chief executive officer Abu Bakar Yusof said the partnership marks an important step in reinforcing Matrade's commitment to sustainable and responsible trade.
"As global markets place greater emphasis on ESG considerations, it is essential that we continue to enhance awareness and ensure that Malaysian exporters remain aligned with evolving international expectations.
"Partnering with RBA enables us to further strengthen our outreach and engagement with global stakeholders," he said in a statement.
Meanwhile, RBA chief executive officer Rob Lederer said the partnership reflects a shared commitment to promoting sustainability and transparency across global supply chains.
He added that it also aims to foster greater awareness of ESG principles among businesses and stakeholders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Five Orang Asli students achieve straight A's in SPM 2025
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results saw five Orang Asli candidates achieve outstanding results, earning straight A+, A, and A- grades in all subjects.
Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said a total of 133 Orang Asli candidates obtained at least a credit, with grades ranging from B+ to C.
"In addition, one candidate from Sekolah Integriti and Henry Gurney School achieved straight A grades, while 24 candidates earned at least a credit and 63 passed with minimum grades.
"For Sekolah Tunas Bakti, one candidate managed to achieve at least a credit in all subjects," he said at a press conference announcing the SPM 2025 results analysis at the Education Ministry (MoE) here today.
Azam added that 1,768 Orang Asli candidates qualified for the SPM 2025 certificate, compared with 1,527 last year.
He said that among candidates from Sekolah Bimbingan Jalinan Kasih, 10 qualified for the SPM 2025 certificate, up from only two in 2024.
"For Sekolah Integriti and Henry Gurney School, 99 candidates qualified in 2025, compared with 94 in 2024.
"A total of 35 candidates from Sekolah Tunas Bakti qualified for the certificate, up from 20 the previous year," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea proposes US$17.3bil extra budget to mitigate Middle East shock
Article:
SEOUL: South Korea proposed on Tuesday a supplementary government budget of US$17.3 billion to support consumers and companies hit by an escalating Middle East war.
A surge in oil prices sparked by the US-Israeli war on Iran has raised growth and inflation risks for Asia's fourth-largest economy, which is also the world's fourth-largest importer of oil, with 70 per cent of its purchases coming from the Middle East.
It is the second extra budget in less than a year under the administration of President Lee Jae Myung, who has vowed to pursue an expansionary fiscal policy to spur economic growth since taking office last June.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cargill boosts Port Klang plant with new line amid rising Asia-Pacific demand
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cargill has announced a multi-million-dollar expansion of its edible oil plant in Port Klang, Malaysia, adding a new speciality fats production line.
The move aims to strengthen the company's portfolio and offer customers a broader range of ingredients for chocolate confectionery, bakery, and dairy products tailored to diverse market and consumer needs.
The expansion comes as Asia Pacific emerges as the fastest-growing region in the global chocolate market, with its share expected to rise from 19.6 per cent in 2025 to 22 per cent by 2030.
Europe continues to dominate as the largest market, while North America maintains steady growth and the Middle East records strong expansion.
Demand is being driven by rising incomes, rapid urbanisation, and evolving consumer preferences, which are boosting consumption of chocolate as well as bakery products such as pastries and other baked goods.
Cargill's Food Southeast Asia, Australia and New Zealand vice president and managing director Kashan Rashid said the new production line at the Port Klang facility supports customers with reliable access to high-quality, versatile speciality fats.
"As food producers navigate evolving cocoa and ingredient markets, our expanded speciality fats portfolio provides an alternative solution with greater flexibility to optimise formulations while maintaining consistent taste and texture.
"This strengthens our ability to work with chocolate, confectionery, bakery and dairy customers as a trusted supplier and innovation partner," he said in a statement.
Cargill operates two edible oil facilities in Malaysia that play a central role in its global speciality fats operations, supplying customers across the Asia Pacific and Europe, the Middle East and Africa (EMEA).
These operations are supported by global sourcing of palm-based and speciality oils such as shea, which are further processed into high-performance ingredients, ensuring a reliable and diversified supply.
The Port Klang site is the first within Cargill's global edible oils network to deploy speciality fats processing technology, strengthening its capability to deliver a broader and more diverse product portfolio.
Cargill's lipid research and development (R&D) centre, also located at the Port Klang plant, enables rapid product and process development with customers, supported by analytical capabilities, performance evaluation, and optimisation.
The expansion builds on a prior US$20 million investment in 2020 to modernise the facility.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested for knife attack and motorcycle arson
Article:
ALOR STAR: Police have arrested a man suspected of injuring another person with a knife before setting the victim's motorcycle ablaze at Jalan Singkir Badong, near Yan, yesterday.
Yan police chief Superintendent Mohamad Hamizi Abdullah said the incident occurred at 7pm and involved a 34-year-old motorcyclist and a 41-year-old suspect.
"The victim was attacked while riding his motorcycle, leading to a struggle in which he sustained a slash wound to his right wrist.
"He managed to escape, leaving his motorcycle behind. The suspect then removed the petrol pipe and set the motorcycle on fire," he said in a statement today.
He added that police arrested the suspect at 8pm on the same day and seized the weapon believed to have been used in the attack.
"Investigations revealed that the suspect was unhappy with the victim for repairing a water drainage pipe he had damaged," he said, adding that the suspect had two prior criminal records related to drugs.
Further investigations will be conducted under Sections 324 and 435 of the Penal Code.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SPM 2025: Record 366,435 candidates qualify for certificate
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A total of 366,435 candidates, or 94.29 per cent, qualified for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 certificate, up from 355,933 candidates, or 94.01 per cent, in 2024.
Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said this represented an increase of 10,502 candidates, or 0.28 percentage points.
"For candidates from Education Ministry (MoE) schools, 329,272 candidates, or 94.51 per cent, qualified in 2025, compared with 319,016 candidates, or 94.20 per cent, in 2024," he said.
He added that this reflected a rise of 10,256 candidates, or 0.31 percentage points, year-on-year.
Azam said 1,878 candidates with special educational needs sat for the 2025 examination, with 1,516, or 80.72 per cent, qualifying, compared with 1,377, or 78.73 per cent, in 2024, marking an increase of 139 candidates, or 1.99 per cent.
Meanwhile, the number of candidates who did not qualify fell to 1,631, or 0.42 per cent, in 2025, down from 1,789, or 0.47 per cent, in 2024.
In total, 413,299 candidates registered for the 2025 SPM, up from 402,918 in 2024.
Candidates may collect their results from their respective schools today. Online result checking is also available via the myresultspm.moe.gov.my website or through SMS, accessible from 10am today until 6pm on April 6.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SPM 2025: BM dips while Islamic Education soars
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Bahasa Melayu (BM) was among 29 of the 95 subjects that recorded a decline in performance in the analysis of the
2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM)
results, based on the Subject Average Grade (GPMP).
Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said 63 subjects showed improved performance, while three remained unchanged compared with SPM 2024.
Among the seven core subjects, three recorded gains, while four saw a drop in performance.
"Islamic Education recorded the highest improvement, rising by 0.79 points to 3.20 in SPM 2025, compared with 3.99 in 2024. Meanwhile, the BM performance dipped by 0.13 points, from 3.72 in 2024 to 3.85 this year," he said at a press conference on the SPM 2025 results analysis at the Education Ministry (MoE) today.
Azam said that elective subjects in the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) stream, including Pure Science and Additional Mathematics, recorded decreases in performance ranging from 0.02 to 0.15 points.
"Among 12 STEM elective subjects in Applied Science and Technology, eight showed improved performance. Electrical and Electronic Engineering Studies recorded the highest increase, rising by 1.64 points compared with 2024," he said.
Meanwhile, Civil Engineering Studies saw a decline in performance, while Computer Science maintained the same results as in SPM 2024.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan schools suspend outdoor activities amid dry spell
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Education Department has instructed all 595 primary and secondary schools in the state to temporarily suspend outdoor activities amid the ongoing dry spell.
State education director Siti Norina Muhamad said circulars were distributed yesterday to all school principals and headmasters as a precautionary measure to safeguard students and staff.
"The directive was issued on March 30 and will remain in effect until further notice," she added.
Norina said all schools had also been advised to closely monitor daily temperatures and follow guidelines issued by the Meteorological Department to prevent heat-related illnesses, including heatstroke.
"If temperatures exceed 35 degrees Celsius for three consecutive days, further measures, including possible school closures or implementation of home-based teaching and learning (PdPR), may be considered," she said.
She added that while the situation in Kelantan remained under control, all outdoor activities in high-risk areas — including annual sports events, camping programmes, and co-curricular activities — were temporarily prohibited.
Norina said that temperatures of 39 to 40 degrees Celsius were classified as extreme.
"So far, no areas in the state have recorded temperatures exceeding 35 degrees Celsius. Kelantan will not implement PdPR at the moment," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Public urged to assist probe into voyeurism at Subang Jaya shopping mall
Article:
SUBANG JAYA: Police have urged members of the public with information to come forward as they intensify efforts to track down a man suspected of recording videos under a woman's skirt at a shopping mall here.
Subang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Wan Azlan Wan Mamat said a report was lodged at 1.16pm on March 29 after a video of the incident began circulating on social media.
"The video shows a male suspect allegedly using a mobile phone to take images beneath a female shopper's skirt.
"Initial investigations found that the video was uploaded on Facebook," he said in a statement today.
Wan Azlan said police are working to identify both the victim and the suspect.
He urged those with information to contact Assistant Superintendent Siti Sarah Johari at 013-209 7958 or 03-78627222.
The case is being investigated for insulting the modest of a person, which can see the ofender sentenced to a maximum of five years in jail, fine, or both upon conviction.
The case is also being investigated under the Minor Offences Act 1955, and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.
Wan Azlan advised the public against sharing or circulating any recordings related to the incident, warning that doing so could compromise the victim's dignity and disrupt ongoing investigations.
It was reported that
police launched an investigation
into a man suspected of taking pictures of women at a retail outlet in Subang Jaya.
A report was lodged regarding the incident, which gained traction after a CCTV recording of the act began circulating online.
The footage has triggered widespread public outrage on social media, with netizens calling for swift enforcement action against the culprit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi urged to quit PKR if he continues public attacks
Article:
KUCHING: The statements and actions of Rafizi Ramli can no longer be taken lightly, says Sarawak Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) secretary Mahmud Epah.
"They clearly show a pattern that is tarnishing PKR's image and undermining the noble aims of Reformasi. I stress that the party's dignity cannot continue to be compromised by polemics from an individual who should be part of the solution, not a source of problems, slander, and betrayal," he said in a statement.
Mahmud added that Rafizi, the former PKR deputy president, should have used the party's democratic channels to communicate his views.
"Otherwise, if his principles no longer align with the party's direction, he should consider leaving. Being an internal critic only burdens the survival of the party," he said.
"The people of Sarawak and all Malaysians want stability. Endless internal polemics will only erode public confidence in PKR's credibility as the backbone of the Madani Government.
"No individual is bigger than the party. Our focus is on nation-building under the leadership of PMX, and anyone obstructing this effort will be firmly opposed," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang records drop in SPM 2025 absenteeism as attendance improves
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang recorded a notable improvement in attendance for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 examination, with absenteeism falling compared with the previous year.
Penang Education Department director Mohammad Dziauddin Mat Saad said a total of 20,057 students registered for SPM 2025, an increase of 804 candidates, or about four per cent, from 2024.
Of these, 18,132 candidates attended the examination, reflecting strong participation.
"The overall attendance stood at 98.91 per cent, with 17,933 candidates present. The number of students absent for all examination papers fell to 199, or 1.09 per cent, compared with 239 last year," he told reporters at the state Education Department today while announcing the SPM 2025 results.
Dziauddin said the reduction in absenteeism highlighted the effectiveness of intervention measures implemented by the department to keep students engaged and committed to sitting for the examination.
"Continuous efforts will be made to further reduce absenteeism and support students in completing their secondary education," he said.
He said that students who were absent cited various reasons, including failure to complete initial registration, early withdrawal from school due to employment, and limited access to schooling after starting work.
"Some students registered but did not participate in lessons or examinations at all due to work commitments," he added.
Dziauddin said that the decline in attendance prompted the department to use a 15-question index to identify causes of absenteeism.
"Counsellors then analysed the responses and implemented appropriate interventions. For example, some students reported needing to work to support their families. In such cases, teachers were informed to make arrangements so the students could still sit for examinations," he said, adding that the issue became more pronounced from Form 4.
Last year's study by the department showed that reasons for
missing SPM 2024
included work commitments and a lack of interest.
Many of these students also had poor class attendance, skipping more days than they attended.
In 2023, a total of 338 candidates did not sit for the SPM examination.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Livestock farmers warn chicken prices could rise amid soaring diesel costs
Article:
MELAKA: The Federation of Livestock Farmers' Associations of Malaysia (FLFAM) says that chicken prices may rise due to higher diesel costs, though the timing of any increase remains uncertain.
According to Kosmo!, FLFAM advisor Datuk Jeffrey Ng said chicken prices in Melaka remained stable for now, as the country was still relying on existing supplies.
"Currently, chicken prices are stable and affordable, but an increase is inevitable due to rising costs, particularly the significant rise in diesel prices. When this will happen, we cannot say for certain, as it depends on supply and operational costs," he told reporters yesterday.
Ng said that should prices rise, discussions would be held with the government to devise a mechanism that would minimise the impact on the public.
"We aim to find the best way to balance costs while avoiding unnecessary pressure on consumers. I also encourage the public to be smart shoppers by comparing prices and choosing traders offering lower rates," he said.
He added that the Economy Ministry had requested the federation to provide a report on the impact of rising diesel prices on chicken and egg costs.
Ng said daily demand for chicken in Peninsular Malaysia currently stood around 2.2 million birds.
A survey at the Melaka Central Market found cleaned chicken selling at between RM8.50 and RM10 per kg.
Trader Hamzah Othman, 62, said prices had remained unchanged since early January, including throughout the Chinese New Year and Aidilfitri celebrations.
"So far, prices have not changed, remaining the same since the start of the year, despite rising operational costs," he said.
A survey of several supermarkets found chicken being sold at lower prices, as low as RM6.29 per kg.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Financial system remains resilient on strong regulatory framework – Bank Negara
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's financial system remained resilient in 2025, supported by a prudent and robust regulatory environment, said Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara).
The central bank said it has focused on four key areas, namely aligning Malaysia's regulatory framework with global standards while adapting them to local needs, and strengthening financial institutions' ability to manage operational disruptions in an increasingly digitised and interconnected environment.
To help financial institutions stay strong and well-prepared for shocks, Bank Negara has continued its efforts to align its regulatory framework with the Basel III standards.
"This in turn protects depositors and supports confidence in the financial system," Bank Negara said in its Annual Report 2025.
In 2025, the central bank issued three Exposure Drafts, namely the Capital Adequacy Framework (Internal Ratings-Based Approach for Credit Risk) (IRB), Capital Adequacy Framework (Counterparty Credit Risk) (CCR) and Interest Rate Risk in the Banking Book (IRRBB), as these policies improve how banks measure and manage risks.
Due to a greater use of digital technology and shared systems, Bank Negara also updated the Risk Management in Technology (RMiT) requirements in line with heightened risks, such as disruptions from cyber incidents and technical failures.
Bank Negara intensified efforts to strengthen trust between financial service providers and consumers with the revised policy, where financial service providers could no longer use a flat rate and/ or the Rule of 78 method for calculating interest or profit for personal financing.
"This means consumers pay interest or profit only on the amount they still owe and are not subject to excess charges for making early repayments," it said.
Similar restrictions on the use of a flat rate and/or the Rule of 78 method would also apply to hire-purchase financing, once the recently enacted Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 takes effect on June 1, 2026.
In the digital payments space, Bank Negara updated the payment card rules, which include stronger authentication methods and new self-service security toggles, allowing cardholders to customise the safety features of their payment card.
"We also plan to set stronger rules on how financial institutions respond to fraud, focusing on proper communication with account holders, to help improve coordination and speed up action across the industry," said Bank Negara.
Additionally, the passing of the Consumer Credit Act 2025 (CCA) marked a key legislative reform aimed at strengthening consumer protection and harmonising consumer credit regulation.
On ensuring crisis preparedness, it said banks need to conduct a deeper analysis of the practicality of their recovery options and more detailed scenario planning to reflect real-world stress events.
Meanwhile, Bank Negara undertook 284 supervisory and enforcement actions in 2025, including imposing monetary penalties totalling RM15.9 million, mainly related to anti-money laundering, technology risk management, prudential standards and foreign exchange policies.
"We acted against nine banks that failed to meet the regulatory expectations we set, including imposing a total penalty of RM5.6 million," said Bank Negara.
Bank Negara continues to uphold transparency through the publication of enforcement actions on its website to deter future misconduct, demonstrating its zero tolerance for serious non-compliances.
Looking ahead to 2026 and beyond, Bank Negara will continue to align prudential standards with global best practices, while tailoring them to Malaysia's unique financial environment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara processes 1.7bil banknotes in 2025, 86pct handled via automated cash centre
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) has processed 1.7 billion banknotes in 2025, compared with 2.5 billion in 2024, with 86 per cent handled through the automated cash centre (ACC) compared with 69 per cent in the previous year.
The central bank said the remaining volume was supported by currency operations undertaken by its regional offices in East Malaysia, and the resiliency of currency operations is further reinforced by the broader cash industry.
"For instance, registered currency processors (RCPs) continue to take on a larger role in supporting day-to-day processing activities such as the collecting, sorting and packing of currency.
"They complement Bank Negara's centralised operations and ensure timely distribution of banknotes to meet public demand," Bank Negara said in its Annual Report 2025 released here today.
Bank Negara said securing cash availability at all times requires a well-coordinated and robust currency operations framework, and it achieves this by maintaining adequate stock levels and striving to process and distribute banknotes efficiently.
It said the shift to centralise all processing activities to a single, purpose-built facility has allowed the central bank to strengthen efficiency, improve risk controls and leverage automation.
"Recirculation is a key component of a sustainable cash ecosystem," Bank Negara said.
By reusing fit banknotes and ensuring coins flow back into the economy, the central bank has reduced waste, lowered production needs and supported a more circular currency ecosystem.
In turn, Bank Negara has issued 1.9 billion banknotes in 2025, with 67 per cent classified as fit banknotes as against 66 per cent in 2024, reflecting the central bank's continued efforts to incorporate sustainability considerations into its currency management operations.
Therefore, this has minimised interruptions in the processing and distribution of banknotes nationwide.
Bank Negara also noted that cash remains in demand even as digital payments gain momentum, as it is easy to use and works even when digital systems are disrupted or connectivity is limited, such as during natural disasters.
"In these instances, the ability to continue transacting sustains confidence in the financial system and reduces stress on the economy.
"Cash enables those in rural areas and the unbanked population to participate in economic activities. For these reasons, strong currency management is a strategic priority for us," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara focuses on value-based intermediation to advance Islamic financial system this year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) is focusing on strengthening value-based intermediation (VBI) capabilities including in sustainability and climate agenda in Islamic financial system this year.
The central bank also highlighted efforts to foster an enabling environment for impactful innovation, and catalyse economic potential through the halal economy and global opportunities.
"Guided by strategic stewardship and collaboration, Bank Negara remains committed to advancing Malaysia's Islamic financial ecosystem, to ensure its progressive evolution delivers lasting benefits for all," it said in its Annual Report 2025 released today.
The central bank said its work centred on three key strategic thrusts in 2025, which are advancing value-based innovation, strengthening the Islamic finance ecosystem, and retaining Malaysia's position as a global leader in the field.
Under Thrust 1, it said Islamic finance is built on the objectives (maqasid) of Shariah and these objectives build on values such as fairness, equitable wealth distribution and shared responsibility. Hence, VBI puts these values into practice.
"For Islamic financial institutions (IFIs), this means to operate and create positive and lasting impacts instead of just focusing on profit.
"In 2024, IFIs intermediated RM148.6 billion into VBI-aligned activities including for Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) and green financing, as well as social finance activities. This shows how VBI places people and the real economy at the centre of financial intermediation," it said.
In building a stronger impact, Bank Negara introduced initiatives to nurture meaningful innovations to support national priorities.
Among the key innovations are greening halal businesses which empower halal SMEs to adopt sustainable practices (RM15 billion grant); risk sharing fund i-CITA to boost use of risk-sharing instruments by Islamic banks (RM100 million funding); and iTEKAD Protection to enhance financial resilience for microentrepreneurs (RM5 million grant).
Under Thrust 2, Bank Negara adopts a whole‑of‑ecosystem approach to support progressive and inclusive growth, which includes providing a conducive regulatory environment, deepening the Islamic financial market, and ensuring Shariah certainty in Islamic financial practices.
It also continued efforts to futureproof the Shariah ecosystem and this is done through research, talent development and public understanding of Islamic finance.
In addition, under Thrust 3, Bank Negara continues its efforts to position Malaysia as a global thought leader in Islamic finance.
"Our priorities for 2025 include strengthening business linkages, promoting mutual Shariah recognition, accelerating innovation and building global capabilities. Through these efforts, Malaysia aims to build a more connected and progressive global Islamic finance ecosystem," it added.
The central bank said throughout 2025, Islamic finance deepened its role in serving Malaysians with Islamic financing rising to 48 per cent of total financing, while takaful participation reached 24.5 per cent.
"This growth reflects how Islamic finance continues to meet the changing financing and protection needs of households and SMEs," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara: Malaysia's e-payment transactions jump 25pct to 18.4bil in 2025
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: E-payment transactions grew by 25 per cent to 18.4 billion, compared to 14.7 billion in 2024, with each Malaysian making 538 e-payments in 2025 on average, a 25 per cent increase from 432 in 2024.
Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) said in its Annual Report 2025 that e-payment transactions rose by an annual average rate (CAGR) of 17 per cent between 2022 and 2025, making Malaysia consistently surpass the Financial Sector Blueprint 2022-2026 target of at least 15 per cent CAGR in average e-payment transactions per capita.
"The growth in 2025 was supported by steady consumption activity, supported by higher retail e-payment transactions by 19 per cent for the year to RM831 billion (2024: RM698.2 billion)," it said in the report released today.
Bank Negara also reported that the higher average e-money transaction size of RM43 in 2025, compared to RM33 in 2024, reflected growing user confidence, not just for daily spending such as transport, food and beverages, but also for higher‑value payments.
On online banking, the central bank said the segment recorded steady growth, with mobile banking continuing to outpace internet banking, as high smartphone penetration enables seamless, on-the-go transactions, making it the preferred online banking channel with a 64 per cent market share.
It said card payments also recorded a healthy growth, with debit card transactions' growth almost doubling the pace of credit card transactions (20.5 per cent versus 10.9 per cent), while the average debit card ticket size was lower by 11.5 per cent to RM69 (2024: RM78).
"Contactless payments also gained traction, growing by 20.3 per cent to two billion transactions in 2025 (2024: 1.7 billion transactions), driven by ease and speed of use.
"DuitNow QR transaction volume doubled to three billion in 2025 (2024: 1.5 billion) mainly due to its convenience and wide merchant acceptance, with almost three million registered DuitNow QR touchpoints across Malaysia as at end-2025 (2024: 2.6 million)," it said.
On the other hand, Bank Negara stated that e-remittance services by licensed remittance service providers surged by 70.1 per cent, reaching RM31.6 billion in 2025, representing 51.8 per cent of outward remittances, with Bangladesh, Indonesia and Myanmar being the top recipient corridors for remittances.
It said the currency exchange transactions undertaken by non-bank currency exchangers through digital means rose by 29.1 per cent to RM7.6 billion (2024: RM5.9 billion), due to more offerings of digital solutions, including through e-payment acceptance and multi-currency prepaid cards.
For this year, Bank Negara said it will continue to pursue efforts to ensure payment and money services are secure and reliable for consumers and businesses, which includes expanding digital payment adoption across all segments of society, while strengthening oversight and fraud prevention to protect users.
"In line with growing global interest towards a tokenised economy, Bank Negara will continue to advance work on asset tokenisation and digital money, including Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), tokenised deposits and ringgit stablecoins, which will be undertaken with the intention to establish a clear regulatory framework.
"This approach provides industry certainty while safeguarding financial stability and consumer protection, which will position Malaysia as a trusted and forward-looking nation for responsible digital finance innovation, hence, translates into real economic gains and supports Malaysia's broader economic transformation," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thailand denies cases of petrol theft involving Malaysian vehicles
Article:
KOTA BHARU: The Thai-Asean Halal Tourism Industry Association (TAHTA) denies allegations that there have been cases of petrol being stolen from the tanks of Malaysian vehicles visiting southern Thailand.
TAHTA president Aida Oujeh said there were indeed cases of petrol theft, but these involved Thai vehicles in Narathiwat during the recent Ramadan.
"Therefore, Malaysians need not be concerned about travelling here, and the police have confirmed that there have been no petrol theft cases involving Malaysian vehicles," she said.
"Fuel supplies in southern Thailand had previously been limited, but the situation has been improving over the past four days," she said.
Earlier, it went viral on social media that there were cases of petrol theft in southern Thailand involving Malaysian vehicles.
The media previously reported that Malaysians had been advised to temporarily postpone travel to Thailand following a shortage of petrol and diesel in the country.
Malaysia's Consul General in Songkhla, Ahmad Fahmi Ahmad Sarkawi, was reported to have said that the advice was specifically for those planning to visit popular destinations such as Hat Yai and Songkhla in southern Thailand during the extended Aidilfitri holiday period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Murder charge for Singaporean in JB court over death of fellow citizen
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, March 31 — A 25-year-old Singaporean man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today for the murder of a fellow countryman in Southkey here last month.
The accused, Muhammad Arif Saiyadi, understood the charge read out by the court interpreter, however no plea was recorded before Magistrate Nabilah Nizam.
According to the charge sheet, the man was charged with murder by causing the death of Chia Hon Chung, 32, at Persiaran Southkey 1, Kota Southkey here at 5.30am on March 14.
For the act, the accused was charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder which provides for the death penalty upon conviction.
The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Siti Aishah Latif, while the accused was unrepresented.
The court set June 3 for mention while awaiting the autopsy report, chemical analysis and deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) results.
Meanwhile, in a separate Magistrate’s Court, another Singaporean man, Shahid Nazareen Shah Phul Bahar Hussain, 33, was fined RM1,700 or four months’ jail for intentionally causing injury to another man.
Magistrate Atifah Hazimah Wahab handed down the sentence after the accused pleaded guilty as soon as the charge was read out to him by the court interpreter.
The man was charged with causing injury to Mohamad Adam Haikal Zakaria, 26, at 4.30am in a toilet area at the same location on the same day.
The accused was charged under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntary causing hurt, which provides for a fine of up to RM2,000 or a maximum year’s imprisonment or both upon conviction.
The accused, who was unrepresented, requested a lower fine amount. However, Deputy Punloc Prosecutor Sarah Siti Aisyah Mustapha Kamal requested that the court impose a reasonable sentence that takes into account the injuries suffered by the victim.
Based on the facts of the case, at 4.30am on March 14, the accused met the victim in the toilet area near the location of the incident before a disagreement broke out.
The accused is said to have punched the victim in the right eye and was then arrested by the police at 12.15am on March 18.

Title: Pontian municipal officer pleads not guilty to cheating charges involving RM62,760 over building application
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, March 31 — A 38 year old municipal administrator claimed trial in the Sessions Court here today on a charge of deceiving a man into handing over a cheque and cash totalling RM62,760 to speed up the application for the construction of a two storey building in Rimba Terjun, Pontian, over the past five years.
The accused, Muhammad Nazri Abu Bakar, who is attached to the Pontian Municipal Council (MPPn), pleaded not guilty to five charges after they were read out by an interpreter before Judge Datuk Ahmad Kamal Arifin Ismail.
Based on the first to fifth charges, the accused is charged with deceiving a 72-year-old man into handing over three cheques and cash totalling RM62,760.
The act was to induce the victim to believe that the accused could hasten the application to build a two-storey building in Rimba Terjun in Pontian, when the accused had not done so.
The offence was allegedly committed by Muhammad Nazri at Batu 36, Jalan Johor in Pontian between November 19, 2021 and May 30, 2022.
The charge was brought under Section 417 of the Penal Code for simple cheating which provides for a prison sentence of up to five years or a fine or both upon conviction. Under the Code, imprisonment is not mandatory.
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) prosecuting officer Muhammad Faizul Kamarulzaman, who prosecuted the case, offered bail of RM20,000 with one surety for the accused.
The accused’s counsel Mazri Muhammad requested that the court grant a minimum bail considering that the accused is supporting his wife and four children, including a child who is autistic.
The court then granted bail of RM10,000 with a local surety and set May 7 for mention and the submission of documents.

Title: Motorcyclist hurt in Yan knife attack, suspect torches bike in rage before Kedah police arrest him
Article:
ALOR SETAR, March 31 — Police have arrested a man suspected of injuring another individual with a knife before setting the victim’s motorcycle ablaze at Jalan Singkir Badong, near Yan, yesterday.
Yan Police chief Supt Mohamad Hamizi Abdullah said the incident occurred at about 7pm and involved a 34-year-old motorcyclist and a 41-year-old suspect.
“The victim was attacked while riding his motorcycle, leading to a struggle in which he sustained a slash wound to his right wrist.
“He managed to escape, leaving his motorcycle behind. The suspect then removed the petrol pipe and set the motorcycle on fire,” he said in a statement today.
He said police arrested the suspect at 8pm on the same day and seized the weapon believed to have been used in the attack.
“Investigation revealed that the suspect was unhappy with the victim for repairing a water drainage pipe he had damaged,” he said, adding that the suspect had two prior criminal records related to drugs, and further investigations will be conducted under Sections 324 and 435 of the Penal Code. — Bernama

Title: Knife attack at Kapar eatery leaves man wounded; police arrest suspect with prior offences
Article:
SHAH ALAM, March 31 — An elderly man was injured on the face and body after being attacked with a knife, believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding at an eatery in the Kapar area in Klang, near here, yesterday.
North Klang District Police chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao said in the incident at about 6.09am, the complainant and the victim, his 69-year-old local friend, were approached by a group of men at the premises.
He said the misunderstanding began when the complainant ignored remarks made by the group, which led one of the suspects to pull out a knife and swing it at the victim.
“Before leaving, the group also issued a criminal threat to shoot the complainant in the near future. The victim is currently in stable condition and receiving treatment at the Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital (HTAR) in Klang,” he said in a statement today.
Vijaya said acting on information, police arrested one of the suspects yesterday, and checks revealed that the individual had eight criminal records and three drug-related records. He has been remanded for three days to assist in investigations.
He said police were actively tracking down the remaining two suspects, and members of the public with information were urged to come forward to the nearest police station or contact the investigating officer, Insp Dayang Siti Aishah Awang Ramli, at 010-5825382.
The case is being investigated under Section 324 and Section 506 of the Penal Code. — Bernama

Title: Penang DOSH clamps down on Bukit Minyak site after worker killed by falling plywood from crane
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 31 — The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) Penang has issued a Prohibition Notice and a stop-work order to the employer at a construction site in the Bukit Minyak Industrial Park following a fatal accident recently.
Its director, Hairozie Asri, said preliminary investigations found that a load of plywood being lifted by a mobile crane became dislodged and fell onto the victim, causing him to fall from a height of approximately six metres.
He said the foreign worker later died at Hospital Seberang Jaya on March 24 due to severe injuries sustained in the fall.
“The accident occurred at the construction site on March 19. The victim, an employee of a subcontractor, was carrying out roofing installation works at the roof slab level when the mishap occurred at about 10.40 am.
“The accident clearly reflects a serious failure in workplace safety management,” he said in a statement today.
Hairozie added that the parties involved have been ordered to immediately cease operations until all safety requirements are fully complied with. — Bernama

Title: BNM says new Consumer Credit Commission will oversee BNPL sector amid rising debt and rapid expansion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The establishment of the Consumer Credit Commission is timely and important in safeguarding consumers through the regulation and supervision of previously unregulated nonbank credit and credit service providers, including buy now pay later (BNPL) providers.
Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) said in the BNPL segment, outstanding exposures continued to be small at 0.3 per cent of total household debt, although its rapid expansion warrants close monitoring.
“The establishment of the commission will bring previously unregulated non-bank credit providers and credit service providers, including BNPL providers, under a formal regulatory and supervisory framework.
“This is expected to strengthen existing consumer protection and oversight efforts,” it said its Financial Stability Review for the second half of 2025 (2H 2025).
BNM said the continued expansion of BNPL schemes remain a key area of surveillance for the central bank.
It said the total volume and value of BNPL transactions increased to 140.3 million transactions and RM11.9 billion respectively in 2H 2025 compared to 102.6 million transactions and RM9.3 billion respectively in 1H 2025.
“Correspondingly, total outstanding BNPL debt increased to RM4.9 billion as of end- December 2025 from RM3.8 billion in June 2025.
“Growth continues to be primarily driven by a sharp rise in active BNPL users, with active accounts reaching 7.5 million (June 2025: 6.5 million),” it said.
BNM added that the share of overdue BNPL debt was stable at 3.2 per cent of total outstanding BNPL debt (June 2025: 3.2 per cent), remaining below levels observed during the early stages of the segment’s emergence of six per cent in March 2023. — Bernama

Title: SPM 2025 levels up as results hit record high, more candidates earn certificates, grade average improves
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, March 31 — The 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination results recorded the best achievement with a National Grade Average (GPK) index of 4.42 points compared to 4.49 points in 2024.
Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said that a total of 366,435 candidates or 94.29 per cent were eligible to be awarded the SPM certificate in 2025 compared to 355,933 candidates in 2024.
“This figure has shown an increase of 10,502 candidates or 0.28 per cent.
“Millions of congratulations to all teachers whose efforts have been expressed in the form of SPM results,” he said in a press conference announcing the 2025 SPM results here today.
Mohd Azam said the analysis of the 2025 SPM examination results showed encouraging achievements of candidates, thus reflecting the effectiveness of the Ministry of Education (MOE)’s continuous efforts in providing access to inclusive and quality education in supporting the aspirations of Madani Malaysia. — Bernama

Title: Red Honda Civic chase at KLIA ends with arrest of local man carrying 31 criminal records
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, March 31 — A police car chase in the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) area yesterday led to the arrest of a local man with 31 prior criminal records.
KLIA police chief ACP M. Ravi said the 30-year-old suspect rammed several public vehicles in an attempt to escape while being pursued by a mobile patrol vehicle (MPV) in the incident at 4.30pm.
He said MPV personnel conducting crime prevention patrols around the KLIA area attempted to stop a red Honda Civic, but the driver refused to pull over for inspection.
“The driver attempted to flee towards the KLIA terminal at Level 3, ramming five public vehicles along the route before being successfully apprehended near the scene,” he said in a statement here today.
Ravi added that checks revealed the man has 31 prior criminal records, as well as one drug-related offence for which he is still wanted by the police.
He said the case is being investigated under Sections 186 and 427 of the Penal Code for obstructing a public servant in the discharge of duties and committing mischief, as well as under Section 15(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and related traffic offences.
He added that the suspect will be brought before the Sepang Court today for a remand application.
Yesterday, a video of the car chase between a police vehicle and a car at KLIA went viral on social media, shocking the public. — Bernama

Title: Johor KPDN intercepts suspicious lorry, seizes 800 litres of illicit diesel worth RM20,716
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, March 31— The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) foiled an attempt to divert 800 litres of diesel in an operation at a petrol station near the Pasir Gudang Highway here, yesterday.
Johor KPDN director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said in a statement that the Enforcement Patrol Unit (URP) detected a suspicious lorry and acted to detain and inspect the vehicle, at about 2pm.
She said the inspection found that the lorry had been modified with two pump units that were connected directly from the vehicle’s original tank to an additional ‘Intermediate Bulk Container’ (IBC) tank in the storage area.
The IBC, she said, is believed to contain about 800 litres of diesel fuel.
Lilis Saslinda said the lorry driver, in his 40s, failed to produce valid documents including a special permit for storing or purchasing diesel fuel and a statement was taken for investigation purposes.
She said the total value of the seizure was estimated at RM20,716 and the case is being investigated under the Control of Supplies Act 1961. — Bernama

Title: Hannah Yeoh visits KLCC with mayor to check on unlicensed ‘photo touts’ preying on tourists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh last night visited the area around the Petronas Twin Towers and Suria KLCC with Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri TPr Fadlun Mak Ujud to check on the presence of unlicensed ‘photo touts’ operating at the landmark site.
Yeoh said she spent more than an hour there and witnessed first-hand how the touts approached tourists to offer photography services.
“Our priority is to ensure that visitors enjoy a safe experience and are not exploited. Safety considerations remain the key factor in our response,” she wrote in a Facebook post.
Her visit follows a recent incident involving unlicensed photographers at KLCC, where 18 individuals were detained following a scuffle with a tourist.
Authorities have pledged tighter enforcement, with Yeoh earlier announcing plans to install more closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras and increase patrols in the area.

Title: Police confirm 10 arrests in Kulim factory trespass case, including journalist, amid tensions over migrant labour
Article:
ALOR SETAR, March 31 — Police have confirmed the arrest of 10 men, including a portal journalist, to assist in investigations into alleged trespassing at a factory construction site in the Kulim Hi-Tech Park (KHTP), Kulim last Wednesday.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said eight of them were detained last Saturday, while two others were arrested yesterday.
He said all of them, aged in their 20s and 40s, were detained and are being investigated under Section 447 of the Penal Code for criminal trespass on private property or land.
“On March 26, at about 3.15pm, an estimated 10 to 12 individuals in eight cars forced their way into the factory site, which is under construction.
“Two security guards on duty attempted to prevent them from entering the premises, but were ignored. They were dissatisfied and questioned the hiring of foreign workers when many locals are still eligible for jobs there,” he said when contacted by Bernama tonight.
He said the eight individuals detained earlier have been released on police bail, while the two arrested yesterday will be brought to court today for a remand order.
The media previously reported that a journalist from Tamil news portal
Thinathanti
, Kalidas Subramaniom, was detained by police at the Kulim district police headquarters (IPD) in connection with alleged trespassing while covering issues related to undocumented migrants. — Bernama

Title: With crude prices soaring, Petronas steps up monitoring and calls for smarter energy use across the nation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) continues to closely monitor the nation’s fuel supply to help safeguard nationwide stability, amid the ongoing West Asia crisis.
In a statement yesterday, Petronas said that, as the duration and full magnitude of the war in Iran remain uncertain, it strongly advocates more efficient energy consumption by both industry and the public.
The national oil company also advises members of the public to avoid panic buying and hoarding of fuel as this would worsen the impact of this crisis.
It said that even though Malaysia is an oil-producing nation, it is not fully insulated from the impact of the crisis.
